task_completion.description: GIMP is open and displays the target landscape image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible, showing a mountain or forest landscape image on the main canvas.

task_completion.description: A selection tool is activated from the toolbox.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A selection tool (such as Rectangle Select, Ellipse Select, or Free Select) is highlighted in the GIMP toolbox.

task_completion.description: A specific region of the image is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashed selection outline ('marching ants') is visible on the image canvas, enclosing a portion of the landscape.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates the menus to copy and paste the selection as a new layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Edit' menu is open, showing interaction with 'Copy', or navigating through 'Edit' -> 'Paste as' -> 'New Layer'.

task_completion.description: A new layer containing the selected region is successfully created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers dialog (usually on the right side) shows at least two layers. A new layer (often named 'Pasted Layer' or 'Clipboard') appears above the original background layer, and its thumbnail displays the isolated copied region.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse cursor movements, tool selections, and menu dropdowns being actively used, rather than terminal windows executing ImageMagick or other CLI commands.

integrity.description: The agent performed the operations on the provided Unsplash landscape image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being manipulated is a real mountain/forest landscape (800x533 pixels), not a blank canvas or a different placeholder image.

integrity.description: The layer was created via standard GUI tools rather than automated scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the manual steps of selecting, copying, and pasting, without the use of the GIMP Python-Fu/Script-Fu console to bypass the UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses a mountain/forest landscape image from Unsplash (800x533 pixels). The environment is GIMP 2.10. The required action 'Paste as New Layer' is natively accessible via the 'Edit -> Paste as -> New Layer' menu path. Success requires verifying that a new layer containing the duplicated content from the selection appears in the XCF layer structure, which is visually verifiable by checking the Layers dialog for a new entry above the background layer.
